pattern: Deleting a node splits the tree into its children's subtrees plus, for every non-root node, everything outside its own subtree, so one bottom-up pass computing subtree sizes is enough to score every node in linear time.
hints: Build a children list from the parents array, then compute every node's subtree size with a single bottom-up pass, processing nodes in an order where every child is handled before its parent.

A node's score multiplies together the sizes of the pieces left behind after deleting it: each child's subtree size, and -- only when the node isn't the root -- the count of every node NOT in its own subtree.
Track a running maximum score and how many nodes tie for it while looping over every node once.

edge_inputs.description: minimum possible tree size of two nodes
a star graph, the root with ten direct children
an unbalanced tree where the root's score competes with a deep node's
two different nodes tie for the highest score
a long chain with one extra branch near the end

stress.description: random tree built by attaching each node to a uniformly random earlier node
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 100000))
    parents = [-1] + [rng.randint(0, i - 1) for i in range(1, n)]
    return [parents]
